_metadata.description: ADC intelligent design rules: diseaseâ†’antigenâ†’binderâ†’linkerâ†’payloadâ†’conjugation decision logic, compatibility matrix, constraints, and safety guardrails

disease_antigen_map._note: Maps disease categories to recommended target antigens with clinical evidence tiers. T1=Approved ADC exists, T2=Phase 2/3, T3=Research/Emerging.

sle.strategy: B-cell depletion via low-toxicity ADC or naked Ab

autoimmune._design_note: ADCs for autoimmune must use LOW-potency payloads (immunomodulators, NOT cytotoxins) or conditional-release linkers to avoid immunosuppression toxicity.

glioblastoma.strategy: BBB-penetrant ADC or intrathecal; low-DAR preferred for safety

neuroblastoma.strategy: Pediatric safety profile critical; low potency payloads preferred

fibrosis.strategy: Stromal-targeted ADC for desmoplastic tumors or systemic fibrosis

antigen_properties._note: Key properties of each antigen that drive downstream design choices.

HER2.evidence_note: Density highly variable by HER2 status: IHC3+ ~1e6 copies/cell; HER2-low IHC1+ ~5e4 copies/cell. Internalization moderate and saturable at high Ab concentrations. Heterogeneity: significant intratumoral in HER2-low setting.
HER2.internalization_mechanism: Clathrin-mediated endocytosis (CME); macropinocytosis at high antibody occupancy (C)
HER2.recycling_after_internalization: Partial recycling to surface (25â€“40%); remainder targeted for lysosomal degradation (C)
HER2.shedding_rate: Low (p95-HER2 shed ECD fragment; not major sink) (C)
HER2.biomarker_assay: IHC + FISH (HER2+: IHC 3+ or IHC 2+/FISH+); HER2-low: IHC 1+ or 2+/FISH-; FDA CDx: PATHWAY HER2 (Ventana)
HER2.proliferation_sensitivity: Moderate â€” bystander-capable payloads (DXd) preferred for HER2-low/heterogeneous tumors
HER2.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) high_variable density and Clinical (C) validation of moderate internalization via T-DXd DESTINY-Breast01. Internalized via Clathrin-mediated endocytosis (CME); macropinocytosis at high antibody occupancy (C).

TROP-2.evidence_note: High density in TNBC (~83% express), urothelial carcinoma. Rapid internalization (t1/2 ~30 min). Low heterogeneity. Validated by IHC in pivotal ASCENT trial.
TROP-2.internalization_mechanism: Rapid constitutive clathrin-mediated endocytosis (CME) + lipid raft-mediated (C)
TROP-2.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily lysosomal degradation (>80% degraded, minimal recycling) (C)
TROP-2.shedding_rate: Low â€” minimal soluble TROP-2 ectodomain shedding (C)
TROP-2.biomarker_assay: IHC (TROP-2 H-score â‰Ą100 for patient selection in some trials); no FDA CDx approved for sacituzumab govitecan
TROP-2.proliferation_sensitivity: Moderate â€” TOP1i (DXd/SN-38) active in all phases
TROP-2.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via ASCENT sacituzumab. Internalized via Rapid constitutive clathrin-mediated endocytosis (CME) + lipid raft-mediated (C).

Nectin-4.evidence_note: High density in urothelial carcinoma (~69% high expression). Rapid internalization. Very low adult normal tissue expression (absent in most non-epithelial tissues).
Nectin-4.biomarker_assay: IHC (>25% tumor cells positive); no FDA CDx currently required for Padcev
Nectin-4.proliferation_sensitivity: Moderate â€” tubulin inhibitor (MMAE); requires proliferating cells
Nectin-4.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via EV-201. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (C).

FRÎ±.evidence_note: Validated in Phase 3 MIRASOL trial for ovarian cancer.
FRÎ±.internalization_mechanism: Receptor-mediated endocytosis (GPI-anchored; clathrin-independent, lipid raft pathway). Internalization is relatively slow but constitutive. (C)
FRÎ±.recycling_after_internalization: Significant recycling (~50% recycled within 4h). GPI-anchor promotes recycling endosome routing. (C)
FRÎ±.shedding_rate: Low â€” minimal soluble FRÎ± detected in serum of most patients. (C)
FRÎ±.biomarker_assay: IHC (FRÎ± â‰Ą75% tumor cells at 2+ intensity for mirvetuximab MIRASOL trial); VENTANA anti-FR alpha (SP130) RxDx is FDA-approved CDx.
FRÎ±.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” DM4 payload is cell-cycle dependent but FRÎ± recycling ensures continuous payload delivery.
FRÎ±.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate_to_high density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via clinical data. Internalized via Receptor-mediated endocytosis (GPI-anchored; clathrin-independent, lipid raft pathway). Internalization is relatively slow but constitutive. (C).

Tissue Factor.evidence_note: High density in cervical cancer. Rapid constitutive and ligand-induced internalization. Normal expression in subendothelial tissue and coagulation cascade (risk of bleeding/coagulation disorders).
Tissue Factor.internalization_mechanism: Rapid constitutive and ligand-induced endocytosis (coagulation cascade activation triggers internalization) (C)
Tissue Factor.biomarker_assay: IHC (â‰Ą25% TF+ tumor cells for Tivdak; FDA CDx: VENTANA SP295 anti-TF RxDx)
Tissue Factor.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Clinical (C) validation of moderate internalization via Tisotumab vedotin innovaTV 204. Internalized via Rapid constitutive and ligand-induced endocytosis (coagulation cascade activation triggers internalization) (C).

CD30.evidence_note: High density on Reed-Sternberg cells (~5,000 copies/cell in HL). Low heterogeneity. Rapid receptor-mediated internalization confirmed in vitro and in vivo.
CD30.shedding_rate: Moderate â€” soluble CD30 (sCD30) shed by metalloprotease ADAM10/17; elevated sCD30 in serum is prognostic but does not significantly affect BV efficacy (C)
CD30.biomarker_assay: IHC (â‰Ą10% CD30+ tumor cells required for HL/ALCL; FDA CDx: CD30 (Dako/Agilent))
CD30.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via BV HL pivotal. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (TNFR superfamily pathway) (C).

CD33.evidence_note: Moderate density (~10,000 copies/cell on AML blasts). Rapid internalization. Expressed on normal myeloid progenitors â€” direct cause of myelosuppression with GO.
CD33.internalization_mechanism: Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (Siglec family; lectin-like internalization) (C)
CD33.recycling_after_internalization: Rapid internalization AND rapid recycling to surface â€” requires pulse dosing strategy (Mylotarg fractionated dosing rationale) (C)
CD33.shedding_rate: Low (some shedding but not a major pharmacological sink) (C)
CD33.biomarker_assay: IHC or flow cytometry (>30% AML blasts CD33+; no strict cutoff required for GO)
CD33.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” calicheamicin (Mylotarg payload) is cell-cycle independent
CD33.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via Mylotarg Phase III. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (Siglec family; lectin-like internalization) (C).

CD79b.evidence_note: Moderate density on mature B-cells (~2,000-12,000 copies/cell). Rapid internalization via BCR complex. Low heterogeneity in DLBCL. Restricted to B-cell lineage.
CD79b.internalization_mechanism: Rapid internalization via B-cell receptor (BCR) complex co-endocytosis; clathrin-mediated (C)
CD79b.biomarker_assay: IHC (VENTANA anti-CD79b; >25% tumor cells); flow cytometry for B-cell lymphomas
CD79b.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via polatuzumab POLARIX. Internalized via Rapid internalization via B-cell receptor (BCR) complex co-endocytosis; clathrin-mediated (C).

CD19.evidence_note: Moderate-high density on B-cell malignancies (20,000-200,000 copies/cell). High internalization. Expressed on all B-cell stages; B-cell aplasia is on-target toxicity.
CD19.internalization_mechanism: Co-internalization with BCR complex (clathrin-mediated); also trogocytosis (C)
CD19.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry or IHC (>20% CD19+ for B-ALL/DLBCL)
CD19.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate_to_high density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via loncastuximab DLBCL. Internalized via Co-internalization with BCR complex (clathrin-mediated); also trogocytosis (C).

BCMA.evidence_note: Moderate density on myeloma cells (~5,000-50,000 copies/cell). High internalization rate. Restricted to plasma cells and a subset of B-cells in normal tissue.
BCMA.internalization_mechanism: Clathrin-mediated endocytosis; APRIL/BAFF ligand-induced internalization increases rate (C)
BCMA.shedding_rate: High â€” BCMA is actively shed by ADAM10/17; soluble BCMA (sBCMA) is a resistance biomarker. Elevated sBCMA associates with belantamab mafodotin resistance. (C)
BCMA.biomarker_assay: IHC or flow cytometry (>50% plasma cells BCMA+); sBCMA serum level is emerging biomarker. FDA CDx: BCMA IHC (pending for several agents)
BCMA.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” auristatin F (belantamab) is mitosis-dependent; bystander effect absent (MMAF)
BCMA.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via belantamab DREAMM-2. Internalized via Clathrin-mediated endocytosis; APRIL/BAFF ligand-induced internalization increases rate (C).

HER3.evidence_note: HER3 (ErbB3) is overexpressed in various solid tumors (NSCLC, breast, gastric). Patritumab deruxtecan (HER3-DXd) shows significant activity in EGFR-mutated NSCLC. Key challenge: low intrinsic kinase activity and significant recycling.
HER3.internalization_mechanism: Rapid internalization via HER2/HER3 heterodimer (requires HER2 co-expression for efficient endocytosis) (I)
HER3.recycling_after_internalization: Significant recycling (HER3 has very low intrinsic kinase activity; recycling > degradation) (I)
HER3.biomarker_assay: IHC (HER3 expression by H-score; no standardized CDx); HER3 is ubiquitously expressed making patient selection challenging
HER3.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Rapid internalization via HER2/HER3 heterodimer (requires HER2 co-expression for efficient endocytosis) (I).

B7-H3.evidence_note: Broad overexpression in solid tumors (neuroblastoma, SCLC, prostate). Ifinatamab deruxtecan (DS-7300) shows robust clinical activity. Favorable therapeutic window due to low normal tissue expression.
B7-H3.biomarker_assay: IHC (H-score â‰Ą100 typically used); no FDA CDx currently
B7-H3.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Moderate â€” clathrin-mediated endocytosis (I).

DLL3.evidence_note: DLL3 expressed in >80% SCLC, LGD (large-cell neuroendocrine), and some glioblastoma. Rovalpituzumab tesirine (Rova-T, SC16LD6.5): Phase 3 TAHOE trial failed â€” excess toxicity from PBD payload at high DAR (DAR 2). Critical failure case: overshooting potency + off-tumor normal tissue expression in neural tissue. Tarlatamab (DLL3Ă—CD3 bispecific) FDA-approved 2024 for SCLC.
DLL3.internalization_mechanism: Moderate-to-rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis. DLL3 is a non-canonical Delta-like Notch ligand; internalization is ligand-independent. Expressed on cell surface as a cis-inhibitor of Notch. (I)
DLL3.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily degraded in lysosomes (not recycled); high lysosomal enzyme activity in SCLC cells promotes efficient payload release (I)
DLL3.shedding_rate: Low â€” transmembrane protein; no significant ectodomain shedding detected in serum (I)
DLL3.biomarker_assay: IHC (DLL3 >25% tumor cells by Ventana SP347 assay); SCLC has near-universal DLL3 positivity (~80â€“90% of SCLC tumors). No FDA CDx required for tarlatamab (bispecific).
DLL3.proliferation_sensitivity: LOW â€” SCLC is fast-proliferating (highly mitotic), but DXd/PBD payloads (cell-cycle independent) may be preferred to avoid heterogeneity issues. Rova-T (PBD-based) failed due to selectivity issues at high potency.
DLL3.design_logic: [Tier 3 (Failed)] Based on Inferred (I) low_to_moderate density and Inferred (I) low internalization. Internalized via Moderate-to-rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis. DLL3 is a non-canonical Delta-like Notch ligand; internalization is ligand-independent. Expressed on cell surface as a cis-inhibitor of Notch. (I).

MET.evidence_note: MET (c-Met) is overexpressed in NSCLC (~25% high expression), gastric (~20%), HCC. Telisotuzumab vedotin (ABBV-399) is a leading MET-MMAE ADC. HGF-induced internalization is rapid. Liver/kidney expression requires careful monitoring.
MET.internalization_mechanism: Rapid ligand (HGF)-induced clathrin-mediated endocytosis with subsequent lysosomal degradation (CBL-mediated ubiquitination). Constitutive internalization in MET-amplified tumors (ligand-independent). Internalization rate: HIGH when activated. (I)
MET.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily degraded (CBL E3 ligase drives lysosomal targeting; MET is a 'disposable' receptor with minimal recycling after activation) (I)
MET.shedding_rate: Moderate â€” ADAM10/17 cleave MET ectodomain releasing soluble MET (sMET); sMET can act as partial antigen sink but not clinically significant in most contexts (I)
MET.biomarker_assay: IHC (MET 3+ by VENTANA MET SP44 for telisotuzumab vedotin; MET amplification by NGS/FISH for METex14 skip mutations); MET/CEP7 ratio >2.0 = amplification. FDA CDx: VENTANA MET SP44 IHC
MET.proliferation_sensitivity: Moderate â€” MMAE (telisotuzumab) requires mitotic cells; MET-amplified NSCLC can be rapidly proliferating
MET.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid ligand (HGF)-induced clathrin-mediated endocytosis with subsequent lysosomal degradation (CBL-mediated ubiquitination). Constitutive internalization in MET-amplified tumors (ligand-independent). Internalization rate: HIGH when activated. (I).

CD22.evidence_note: Moderate-high density on B-cells/B-ALL (10,000-100,000 copies/cell). Rapid constitutive internalization and recycling. Low heterogeneity in B-ALL. Restricted to B-cell lineage.
CD22.internalization_mechanism: Constitutive receptor-mediated endocytosis (lectin-type; internalizes B-cell receptor complex) (C)
CD22.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily degraded in lysosomes; some recycling (C)
CD22.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (>20% CD22+ blasts in B-ALL); IHC for lymphoma
CD22.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via besylomab ALL. Internalized via Constitutive receptor-mediated endocytosis (lectin-type; internalizes B-cell receptor complex) (C).

CLDN6.evidence_note: Preclinical studies of CLDN6-23-ADC show rapid internalization and potent efficacy. Target for Toripalimab-ADC (JS108).
CLDN6.internalization_mechanism: Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis. Unlike CLDN18.2, CLDN6 is often more accessible on the cell surface rather than sequestered in tight junctions in certain tumor types. (I)
CLDN6.shedding_rate: Very low â€” four-pass transmembrane protein. (I)
CLDN6.biomarker_assay: IHC (CLDN6 expression in ovarian, testicular, and lung cancers).
CLDN6.design_logic: [Tier 4 (Emerging)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate_to_high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis. Unlike CLDN18.2, CLDN6 is often more accessible on the cell surface rather than sequestered in tight junctions in certain tumor types. (I).

GPC3.evidence_note: GPC3 is highly expressed in HCC (~70%), squamous NSCLC (~40%). Internalization is rapid but recycling is significant. Codrituzumab failed as naked mAb, but GPC3-ADCs (e.g., HKT288) are in development.
GPC3.internalization_mechanism: Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis (GPC3 is GPI-anchored heparan sulfate proteoglycan; internalization requires co-receptor engagement or clathrin-independent pathway via glypican shedding of GPI anchor) (I)
GPC3.recycling_after_internalization: Significant recycling (GPI-anchored proteins are preferentially recycled via Rab11 recycling endosomes); ~50â€“60% recycled; rest degraded (I)
GPC3.shedding_rate: Moderate â€” heparanase cleaves GPC3 from cell surface; shed GPC3 detectable in serum of HCC patients. Elevated serum GPC3 (>2 ng/mL) predicts poor prognosis but does not significantly reduce membrane-bound target. (I)
GPC3.biomarker_assay: IHC (GPC3 â‰Ą2+ in â‰Ą20% tumor cells for HCC); serum GPC3 as diagnostic marker (sensitivity ~60%, specificity ~95% for HCC vs cirrhosis). No FDA CDx approved.
GPC3.proliferation_sensitivity: Moderate â€” most GPC3 ADCs use MMAE (mitosis-dependent); however HCC proliferates variably
GPC3.design_logic: [Tier 4 (Emerging)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis (GPC3 is GPI-anchored heparan sulfate proteoglycan; internalization requires co-receptor engagement or clathrin-independent pathway via glypican shedding of GPI anchor) (I).

Nectin-2.internalization_mechanism: Internalization required for activity; similar to Nectin-4, it undergoes clathrin-mediated endocytosis. (I)
Nectin-2.evidence_note: Target for various solid tumor ADCs (e.g., SKB410/MK-3120).
Nectin-2.design_logic: [Tier 4 (Emerging)] Based on Inferred (I) Moderate (I) and Inferred (I) High (I). Internalized via Internalization required for activity; similar to Nectin-4, it undergoes clathrin-mediated endocytosis. (I).

PTK7.evidence_note: PTK7 (Pseudo-tyrosine kinase 7) is overexpressed in TNBC, NSCLC, and ovarian cancer. Cofretuzumab pelidotin (PF-06647020) is a leading ADC. Rapid internalization and moderate density, but high heterogeneity and normal tissue expression require careful dosing.
PTK7.internalization_mechanism: Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Pseudo-tyrosine kinase 7; Wnt pathway co-receptor). (I)
PTK7.shedding_rate: Moderate â€” soluble PTK7 shed by ADAM17. (I)
PTK7.biomarker_assay: IHC (PTK7 expression in TNBC, NSCLC, ovarian).
PTK7.design_logic: [Tier 3 (Failed)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Pseudo-tyrosine kinase 7; Wnt pathway co-receptor). (I).

CDH6.evidence_note: CDH6 (Cadherin-6) is overexpressed in ovarian and renal cancers. Raludotatug deruxtecan (DS-6000) shows high ORR in ovarian cancer. Rapid internalization and low normal tissue expression (except kidney) make it a strong target.
CDH6.biomarker_assay: IHC (CDH6 expression in ovarian and renal cancers).
CDH6.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (Cadherin-6; clathrin-mediated). (I).

CEACAM5.evidence_note: CEACAM5 (CEA) is highly expressed in CRC, gastric, and NSCLC. Tusamitamab ravtansine (SAR408701) is a leading ADC. Moderate shedding and partial recycling are challenges, but high density and tumor-restricted expression (apical) provide a window.
CEACAM5.shedding_rate: Moderate â€” soluble CEA shed into bloodstream (serum CEA a tumor marker); shed CEA can act as antigen sink at very high tumor burden (>1000 ng/mL). However, membrane-bound CEACAM5 is still well-maintained. (I)
CEACAM5.internalization_mechanism: Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis after antibody cross-linking (CEACAM5 is GPI-anchored in many cells but transmembrane isoform on cancer cells); lipid raft-mediated pathway; internalization rate varies by tumor type (I)
CEACAM5.recycling_after_internalization: Moderate recycling (~40â€“50%) in colorectal cancer cell lines; remainder degraded (I)
CEACAM5.biomarker_assay: IHC (CEACAM5 â‰Ą2+ in â‰Ą50% tumor cells for tusamitamab ravtansine trial); serum CEA for disease monitoring (>5 ng/mL = elevated). FDA CDx: not yet approved for any CEACAM5 ADC.
CEACAM5.proliferation_sensitivity: Moderate â€” DM4-based ADCs (tusamitamab) are mitosis-dependent
CEACAM5.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) low internalization. Internalized via Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis after antibody cross-linking (CEACAM5 is GPI-anchored in many cells but transmembrane isoform on cancer cells); lipid raft-mediated pathway; internalization rate varies by tumor type (I).

MSLN.evidence_note: Mesothelin is highly expressed in mesothelioma and ovarian cancer. Internalization is slow, and shedding is high, creating a significant antigen sink. Anetumab ravtansine (anti-MSLN-DM4) and other ADCs are in clinical trials.
MSLN.internalization_mechanism: POOR â€” GPI-anchored protein primarily on apical/luminal surface; macropinocytosis is limited; SLOW internalization is a major ADC design challenge (I)
MSLN.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily membrane-retained; minimal degradation after internalization (I)
MSLN.shedding_rate: High â€” soluble megakaryocyte potentiating factor (MPF) shed by ADAM proteases; soluble mesothelin in plasma acts as antigen sink (reduces effective dose reaching tumor) (I)
MSLN.biomarker_assay: IHC (mesothelin expression by H-score >50); serum soluble mesothelin (SMRP) as pharmacodynamic biomarker
MSLN.proliferation_sensitivity: High â€” most Mesothelin ADCs use MMAE; poor internalization limits payload delivery efficiency
MSLN.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via POOR â€” GPI-anchored protein primarily on apical/luminal surface; macropinocytosis is limited; SLOW internalization is a major ADC design challenge (I).
MSLN._note: Alias for Mesothelin â€” see that entry for canonical data

MUC1.evidence_note: MUC1 is ubiquitously expressed but aberrantly glycosylated in cancer (TA-MUC1). Massive shedding of CA 15-3 is a major challenge. ADCs targeting TA-MUC1 (e.g., PBD-based) are preferred to avoid normal tissue toxicity and antigen sink.
MUC1.internalization_mechanism: Moderate constitutive endocytosis (MUC1 is a heavily glycosylated mucin; internalization occurs via macropinocytosis and clathrin-mediated pathways; rate depends on glycosylation state â€” aberrantly glycosylated (TA-MUC1) internalizes faster than normal) (I)
MUC1.recycling_after_internalization: Significant recycling (~50â€“60%) â€” MUC1 cytoplasmic domain interacts with Î˛-catenin and multiple signaling pathways; recycling is part of its oncogenic signaling function (I)
MUC1.shedding_rate: HIGH â€” MUC1 ectodomain extensively shed (CA 15-3 antigen is shed MUC1 ectodomain; serum CA 15-3 in breast cancer can be >1000 U/mL). Shed MUC1 acts as significant antigen sink. ADC strategies target membrane-proximal domain or aberrant glyco-epitopes (TA-MUC1) that are less shed. (I)
MUC1.biomarker_assay: IHC (MUC1/EMA â‰Ą1+ expression); serum CA 15-3 for treatment monitoring. For ADC trials: TA-MUC1 specific IHC (VU4H11 or HMFG1 antibody) to distinguish tumor-specific from normal MUC1.
MUC1.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Moderate constitutive endocytosis (MUC1 is a heavily glycosylated mucin; internalization occurs via macropinocytosis and clathrin-mediated pathways; rate depends on glycosylation state â€” aberrantly glycosylated (TA-MUC1) internalizes faster than normal) (I).

MUC16.evidence_note: MUC16 (CA125) is highly expressed in ovarian cancer. Massive shedding of the CA125 domain is the primary barrier. ADCs must target the retained proximal STALK domain (e.g., DMUC5754A) to avoid the antigen sink.
MUC16.shedding_rate: VERY HIGH â€” massive ectodomain shedding (CA125) is the primary challenge. Shed CA125 acts as a massive antigen sink in ovarian cancer (serum CA125 >10,000 U/mL common). ADCs must target the proximal STALK domain (retained after shedding). (I)
MUC16.internalization_mechanism: Moderate â€” transmembrane domain anchors it; EGF-domain binding triggers endocytosis (I)
MUC16.biomarker_assay: Serum CA125 (CA125/MUC16 EIA; used as PD biomarker); IHC for tumor expression. Target: membrane-proximal repeat domain (e.g., DMUC5754A targets retained stalk)
MUC16.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) low internalization. Internalized via Moderate â€” transmembrane domain anchors it; EGF-domain binding triggers endocytosis (I).

ROR1.evidence_note: ROR1 is an oncofetal antigen expressed in CLL, MCL, and various solid tumors (TNBC, lung). Zilovertamab vedotin (VLS-101) shows promising clinical activity. Rapid internalization via caveolae/clathrin-mediated pathways.
ROR1.internalization_mechanism: Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (caveolae-mediated reported in some cell lines; mechanism varies) (I)
ROR1.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (>20% ROR1+) or IHC; ROR1 protein expression correlates with gene expression
ROR1.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (caveolae-mediated reported in some cell lines; mechanism varies) (I).

ROR2.evidence_note: ROR2 is an orphan receptor tyrosine kinase overexpressed in RCC, osteosarcoma, and melanoma. BA3021 (CAB-ROR2-ADC) is a leading candidate. Rapid internalization and tumor-specific expression make it a strong target.
ROR2.internalization_mechanism: Rapid internalization via clathrin-mediated endocytosis, confirmed by functional screening of human antibody libraries. (I)
ROR2.biomarker_assay: IHC for ROR2 expression in RCC and osteosarcoma.
ROR2.design_logic: [Tier 4 (Emerging)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid internalization via clathrin-mediated endocytosis, confirmed by functional screening of human antibody libraries. (I).

ENPP3.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (Ectonucleotide pyrophosphatase/phosphodiesterase 3). (I).

CD70.internalization_mechanism: Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (TNFR superfamily member; rapid after antibody binding). (I)
CD70.biomarker_assay: IHC or flow cytometry (CD70 expression in RCC and lymphoma).
CD70.design_logic: [Tier 3 (Failed)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (TNFR superfamily member; rapid after antibody binding). (I).

CD228.internalization_mechanism: Rapid and efficient internalization in various solid tumor types (melanoma, lung, breast). (I)
CD228.evidence_note: SGN-CD228A (hL49-MMAE) demonstrates that cytotoxic activity is strictly dependent on CD228 internalization.
CD228.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) High (I) and Inferred (I) High (C). Internalized via Rapid and efficient internalization in various solid tumor types (melanoma, lung, breast). (I).

FAP.internalization_mechanism: Internalization confirmed in vitro for FAP-targeting ADCs (e.g., FAP-exatecan), though rate can be influenced by DAR and linker hydrophobicity. (I)
FAP.biomarker_assay: IHC for FAP in tumor stroma/CAFs.
FAP.evidence_note: FAP-exatecan ADC (DAR 8) shows potent activity and reverses immunosuppressive TME.
FAP.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) Moderate (I) and Inferred (I) Moderate (I). Internalized via Internalization confirmed in vitro for FAP-targeting ADCs (e.g., FAP-exatecan), though rate can be influenced by DAR and linker hydrophobicity. (I).

LGR5.evidence_note: Target for BNC101 and other CRC-targeted ADCs.
LGR5.internalization_mechanism: Rapid constitutive clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Leucine-rich repeat-containing G-protein coupled receptor 5; Wnt pathway marker). (I)
LGR5.biomarker_assay: IHC or RNA-ISH (LGR5 expression in CRC).
LGR5.design_logic: [Tier 3 (Failed)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid constitutive clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Leucine-rich repeat-containing G-protein coupled receptor 5; Wnt pathway marker). (I).

LRRC15.internalization_mechanism: Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Leucine-rich repeat containing 15; expressed on tumor-associated fibroblasts and some tumor cells). (I)
LRRC15.biomarker_assay: IHC (LRRC15 expression in osteosarcoma and other solid tumors).
LRRC15.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high (stroma) density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Leucine-rich repeat containing 15; expressed on tumor-associated fibroblasts and some tumor cells). (I).

SLITRK6.internalization_mechanism: Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (SLIT and NTRK-like protein 6). (I)
SLITRK6.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (SLIT and NTRK-like protein 6). (I).

UPK2.internalization_mechanism: Efficient internalization in urothelial cells; identified as a highly specific target for bladder cancer. (I)
UPK2.evidence_note: UPK2-ADC shows potent activity in preclinical bladder cancer models.
UPK2.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) Moderate (I) and Inferred (I) Moderate (I). Internalized via Efficient internalization in urothelial cells; identified as a highly specific target for bladder cancer. (I).

NaPi2b.evidence_note: NaPi2b is a sodium-dependent phosphate transporter highly expressed in ovarian and lung adenocarcinoma (NSCLC). Lifastuzumab vedotin (DNIB0600A) and Upifitamab rilsodotin (UpRi) are key ADCs. Moderate internalization and significant recycling.
NaPi2b.internalization_mechanism: Moderate constitutive endocytosis (Type IIb sodium-phosphate cotransporter; clathrin-mediated). (I)
NaPi2b.recycling_after_internalization: Significant recycling (~40â€“60%) as part of phosphate homeostasis. (I)
NaPi2b.shedding_rate: Very low â€” multi-pass transmembrane protein. (I)
NaPi2b.biomarker_assay: IHC (NaPi2b â‰Ą2+ in â‰Ą10% tumor cells for lifastuzumab trials).
NaPi2b.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Moderate constitutive endocytosis (Type IIb sodium-phosphate cotransporter; clathrin-mediated). (I).

LIV-1.evidence_note: LIV-1 (SLC39A6) is a zinc transporter overexpressed in breast and prostate cancers. Ladiratuzumab vedotin (SGN-LIV1A) is in clinical development. Rapid internalization and low normal tissue expression.
LIV-1.internalization_mechanism: Rapid constitutive endocytosis (SLC39A6 zinc transporter; clathrin-mediated). (I)
LIV-1.biomarker_assay: IHC (LIV-1 â‰Ą2+ in â‰Ą10% tumor cells for ladiratuzumab trials).
LIV-1.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate_to_high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid constitutive endocytosis (SLC39A6 zinc transporter; clathrin-mediated). (I).

GCC.evidence_note: Guanylyl cyclase C (GCC) is highly expressed in colorectal cancer (CRC) and gastric cancer. Indusatumab vedotin (MLN0264) is a leading ADC. Rapid internalization and restricted expression to the apical surface of GI epithelia provide a therapeutic window.
GCC.internalization_mechanism: Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (Guanylyl cyclase C; ligand ST-induced or antibody-induced). (I)
GCC.biomarker_assay: IHC (GCC expression in CRC and gastric cancer).
GCC.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (Guanylyl cyclase C; ligand ST-induced or antibody-induced). (I).

ASCT2.internalization_mechanism: Efficient internalization via clathrin-mediated endocytosis; validated in various tumor types (CRC, NSCLC). (I)
ASCT2.evidence_note: MEDI7247 (ASCT2-targeted ADC) shows rapid internalization and potent antitumor activity.
ASCT2.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) Moderate (I) and Inferred (I) High (I). Internalized via Efficient internalization via clathrin-mediated endocytosis; validated in various tumor types (CRC, NSCLC). (I).

B7-H4.evidence_note: B7-H4 (VTCN1) is overexpressed in breast, ovarian, and endometrial cancers. AZD8205 (anti-B7-H4-DXd) is in clinical development. Low normal tissue expression and moderate internalization make it a promising ADC target.
B7-H4.internalization_mechanism: Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis (B7-H4/VTCN1 is a B7 family checkpoint ligand; internalization rate is moderate â€” ~40â€“60% internalized within 4h) (I)
B7-H4.biomarker_assay: IHC (B7-H4 â‰Ą5% tumor cells for clinical trials; VENTANA B7-H4 SP309 assay); expressed in breast (~25â€“40%), ovarian (~60%), endometrial (~50%). No FDA CDx.
B7-H4.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis (B7-H4/VTCN1 is a B7 family checkpoint ligand; internalization rate is moderate â€” ~40â€“60% internalized within 4h) (I).

CD25.internalization_mechanism: Rapid internalization via the IL-2 receptor complex (clathrin-independent, dynamin-dependent). (I)
CD25.recycling_after_internalization: Low; primarily targeted to late endosomes/lysosomes. (I)
CD25.shedding_rate: Moderate (soluble CD25 can be detected in serum). (I)
CD25.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry or IHC for CD25 (IL-2Ra).
CD25.evidence_note: Target for Camidanlumab tesirine (ADCT-301). Rapid internalization is a hallmark of IL-2R signaling.
CD25.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) Moderate (I) and Inferred (I) High (I). Internalized via Rapid internalization via the IL-2 receptor complex (clathrin-independent, dynamin-dependent). (I).

CD46.internalization_mechanism: Efficient internalization via multiple pathways; identified as a top internalizing target in prostate cancer. (I)
CD46.biomarker_assay: IHC for CD46 (overexpressed in mCRPC and NEPC).
CD46.evidence_note: CD46 targeted by FOR46 ADC shows robust internalization and efficacy in prostate cancer models.
CD46.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) Moderate (I) and Inferred (I) Moderate (I). Internalized via Efficient internalization via multiple pathways; identified as a top internalizing target in prostate cancer. (I).

CD37.internalization_mechanism: Moderate to rapid internalization; tetraspanin family members often facilitate rapid endocytosis upon cross-linking. (I)
CD37.biomarker_assay: IHC or Flow for CD37 in B-cell lymphomas.
CD37.evidence_note: Naratuximab emtansine (IMGN529) relies on CD37 internalization for payload delivery.
CD37.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) Moderate (I) and Inferred (I) High (C). Internalized via Moderate to rapid internalization; tetraspanin family members often facilitate rapid endocytosis upon cross-linking. (I).

CD138.evidence_note: CD138 expressed on >99% myeloma plasma cells, hepatocytes, epithelial cells. High shedding is the primary challenge â€” shed ectodomain captures ADC before tumor delivery. Indatuximab ravtansine (BT062, anti-CD138-DM4): modest single-agent activity. Combination with dexamethasone showed improved response. Belantamab approach (BCMA) may have partially superseded CD138 as myeloma target due to lower shedding.
CD138.internalization_mechanism: Moderate-to-rapid endocytosis (syndecan-1 heparan sulfate proteoglycan; internalized via clathrin-mediated and macropinocytosis pathways; shedding of ectodomain is the major regulatory mechanism) (I)
CD138.shedding_rate: HIGH â€” CD138 ectodomain actively shed by MMP-7 and heparanase into serum; shed syndecan-1 stimulates tumor growth and angiogenesis. Soluble CD138 in myeloma patients: 100â€“3000 ng/mL. Major antigen sink concern. (I)
CD138.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (CD138+ plasma cells, standard myeloma diagnosis); IHC for plasmacytoma/light-chain amyloidosis. Serum syndecan-1 (soluble CD138) as prognostic marker.
CD138.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” plasma cells are largely post-mitotic; cell-cycle independent payloads (DNA alkylators, RNA pol II inhibitors) preferred
CD138.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Moderate-to-rapid endocytosis (syndecan-1 heparan sulfate proteoglycan; internalized via clathrin-mediated and macropinocytosis pathways; shedding of ectodomain is the major regulatory mechanism) (I).

FcRH5.evidence_note: FcRH5 expressed on all B cells and plasma cells, including normal B cells (not myeloma-selective). Cevostamab (FcRH5Ă—CD3) shows activity in heavily pretreated myeloma. ADC format potentially advantageous to reduce off-tumor B-cell depletion vs bispecific. Clinical ADC programs in early development.
FcRH5.internalization_mechanism: Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (FcRH5/FCRL5 is an Fc receptor-like protein; rapid internalization after antibody binding via clathrin-mediated pathway) (I)
FcRH5.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry or IHC (FcRH5 >20% plasma cells); expressed on all B-lineage cells (B cells, plasma cells). Cevostamab (FcRH5Ă—CD3 bispecific) efficacy correlated with FcRH5 expression level.
FcRH5.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” myeloma plasma cells are largely post-mitotic
FcRH5.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (FcRH5/FCRL5 is an Fc receptor-like protein; rapid internalization after antibody binding via clathrin-mediated pathway) (I).

GPRC5D.evidence_note: GPRC5D expressed on malignant plasma cells (>90% myeloma), hair follicles, and skin keratinocytes. FDA-approved: talquetamab (GPRC5DĂ—CD3 bispecific) 2023. Key on-target off-tumor toxicities: dysgeusia, nail changes, skin rash from hair follicle expression. ADC format under development with DM1/MMAE payloads to avoid bispecific-related CRS.
GPRC5D.internalization_mechanism: Rapid constitutive internalization (orphan GPCR; Class C GPCR family; constitutive ligand-independent endocytosis typical of class C GPCRs via clathrin-mediated pathway) (I)
GPRC5D.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily degraded (GPCR downregulation pathway via ubiquitin-proteasome and lysosomal targeting after agonist-independent internalization) (I)
GPRC5D.shedding_rate: Very low â€” seven-pass transmembrane GPCR; no known ectodomain shedding (I)
GPRC5D.biomarker_assay: IHC (GPRC5D â‰Ą10% plasma cells for talquetamab trial); flow cytometry for myeloma cells. GPRC5D protein detected in hair follicles and skin â€” source of dermatological toxicity (rash, nail changes) with talquetamab.
GPRC5D.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” plasma cells are post-mitotic; cell-cycle independent payloads preferred for myeloma
GPRC5D.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid constitutive internalization (orphan GPCR; Class C GPCR family; constitutive ligand-independent endocytosis typical of class C GPCRs via clathrin-mediated pathway) (I).

CLL-1.evidence_note: CLL-1 expressed on >80% AML blasts and >90% leukemic stem cells (LSCs). NOT expressed on normal HSCs (CD34+CD38- hematopoietic stem cells) â€” critical advantage over CD33 and CD123. DCLL9718A (anti-CLL1-PBD ADC): Phase 1 in AML. Key advantage: CLL-1 on LSCs allows stem cell eradication without normal HSC toxicity.
CLL-1.internalization_mechanism: Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (CLEC12A/CLL-1 is a C-type lectin; rapid ligand-induced and constitutive internalization; highly expressed on AML blasts and LSCs (leukemic stem cells)) (I)
CLL-1.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily degraded (lectin-type receptor; rapid lysosomal routing after internalization) (I)
CLL-1.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (CLL-1/CLEC12A >20% AML blasts; expressed on LSCs which are CD34+CD38- â€” important for MRD eradication); IHC for blast infiltration in marrow biopsy
CLL-1.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” DNA-damaging or RNA pol II inhibitors preferred (CLL-1 is expressed on quiescent LSCs that must be targeted)
CLL-1.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (CLEC12A/CLL-1 is a C-type lectin; rapid ligand-induced and constitutive internalization; highly expressed on AML blasts and LSCs (leukemic stem cells)) (I).

PSMA.evidence_note: PSMA (FOLH1) is highly expressed in prostate cancer. Multiple ADCs (e.g., MEDI3726) and radioligands (e.g., Pluvicto) target PSMA. Rapid internalization and high density make it an ideal target.
PSMA.internalization_mechanism: Ligand-induced rapid internalization (clathrin-mediated; PSMA is a Type II membrane protein folate hydrolase) (I)
PSMA.biomarker_assay: IHC (PSMA expression by IHC); PSMA PET-CT (68Ga-PSMA-11 or 18F-DCFPyL) for in vivo imaging; serum PSA for disease monitoring
PSMA.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” PSMA ADCs often use DNA-damaging payloads (cell-cycle independent)
PSMA.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Ligand-induced rapid internalization (clathrin-mediated; PSMA is a Type II membrane protein folate hydrolase) (I).

STEAP1.evidence_note: STEAP1 is overexpressed in prostate cancer and Ewing sarcoma. Vandortuzumab vedotin (RG7450) is a leading ADC. Moderate internalization and high heterogeneity in advanced disease.
STEAP1.internalization_mechanism: Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (six-pass transmembrane ferrireductase; ligand-stimulated and constitutive internalization via clathrin-coated pits) (I)
STEAP1.shedding_rate: Very low â€” multi-pass transmembrane protein; minimal shedding (I)
STEAP1.biomarker_assay: IHC (STEAP1 â‰Ą1+ for vandortuzumab vedotin trials; >50% of prostate cancer cells); flow cytometry for cell lines. No FDA CDx available.
STEAP1.proliferation_sensitivity: Moderate â€” tubulin inhibitors (MMAE) used in vandortuzumab; prostate cancer often slow-proliferating; MMAE limited in G0 cells
STEAP1.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (six-pass transmembrane ferrireductase; ligand-stimulated and constitutive internalization via clathrin-coated pits) (I).

CD20.evidence_note: CD20 expressed in >95% of B-cell lymphomas. Critical challenge for ADCs: classic CD20 mAbs (rituximab, obinutuzumab) are POOR internalizers â€” designed for ADCC/CDC. Successful ADC requires modified internalization-promoting clone. CD20-ADC programs: SAR3419 (anti-CD20-DM4, failed Phase 2 due to poor activity), ADCT-301 (haematologix). Post-CD20 therapy CD20-loss is major resistance barrier.
CD20.internalization_mechanism: VERY SLOW â€” CD20 is a non-internalizing tetraspanin membrane protein in normal B cells; type II antibody (obinutuzumab) promotes more internalization than type I. Rituximab-type mAbs are specifically engineered to NOT internalize (to maximize ADCC/CDC). ADC format requires careful selection of internalizing antibody clone. (I)
CD20.recycling_after_internalization: Minimal internalization and degradation; mostly stays on cell surface (I)
CD20.shedding_rate: Very low â€” integral membrane protein; no ectodomain shedding (I)
CD20.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (CD20+ B-ALL, DLBCL, CLL: >20% CD20+); IHC for tissue lymphoma. CD20 expression can be LOST after rituximab treatment (major resistance mechanism â€” CD20-negative relapse in ~20% B-ALL).
CD20.proliferation_sensitivity: HIGH â€” ADC format for CD20 requires either (1) careful clone selection for internalization, or (2) ADCC-competent IgG1 framework. Cell-cycle independent payloads (PBD, DNA alkylator) better for slow/non-proliferating B-cell lymphomas.
CD20.design_logic: [Tier 3 (Failed)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via VERY SLOW â€” CD20 is a non-internalizing tetraspanin membrane protein in normal B cells; type II antibody (obinutuzumab) promotes more internalization than type I. Rituximab-type mAbs are specifically engineered to NOT internalize (to maximize ADCC/CDC). ADC format requires careful selection of internalizing antibody clone. (I).

CD38.evidence_note: CD38 is nearly universally expressed on myeloma cells. Daratumumab is the standard naked mAb. ADCs (e.g., TAK-169) are designed to overcome daratumumab resistance. Moderate internalization and high density.
CD38.internalization_mechanism: Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis. CD38 is a type II transmembrane glycoprotein with ectoenzyme (NADase/cyclase) activity. Internalization occurs after antibody binding; rate is moderate (~30â€“50% internalized within 4h at 37Â°C). (I)
CD38.recycling_after_internalization: Partial recycling (~30â€“40%); NAD cycling requires surface expression so recycling is maintained (I)
CD38.shedding_rate: Low â€” CD38 is not a major shedding target; limited ectodomain release (I)
CD38.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (CD38+ plasma cells, myeloma standard diagnosis); IHC. FDA-approved CDx: daratumumab (DARZALEX) is standard of care â€” CD38 expression is universal in myeloma.
CD38.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” mostly post-mitotic plasma cells; cell-cycle independent payloads preferred
CD38.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via Moderate clathrin-mediated endocytosis. CD38 is a type II transmembrane glycoprotein with ectoenzyme (NADase/cyclase) activity. Internalization occurs after antibody binding; rate is moderate (~30â€“50% internalized within 4h at 37Â°C). (I).

CD47.evidence_note: CD47 is a 'don't eat me' signal ubiquitously expressed. Magrolimab is a leading naked mAb. ADC development is challenging due to the massive RBC sink and slow internalization. Probody or other selective activation strategies are required.
CD47.internalization_mechanism: SLOW â€” CD47 is a 'don't eat me' signal (integrin-associated protein); minimal spontaneous internalization. Antibody binding triggers phagocytosis by macrophages (ADCP) rather than classical endocytosis by target tumor cells themselves. Limited direct internalization by tumor cell. (I)
CD47.recycling_after_internalization: Minimal (CD47 function is as a macrophage signaling ligand; tumor cell-autonomous internalization is not a primary pathway) (I)
CD47.shedding_rate: Very low â€” integral membrane protein; no significant shedding (I)
CD47.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry or IHC (CD47 ubiquitously expressed on all normal cells and tumor cells â€” not a selective biomarker). RBC expression is major dose-limiting toxicity source (anemia from RBC agglutination).
CD47.proliferation_sensitivity: N/A â€” primary mechanism of CD47 antibodies is ADCP by macrophages, not direct cytotoxic payload delivery. ADC concept for CD47 is novel/experimental.
CD47.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via SLOW â€” CD47 is a 'don't eat me' signal (integrin-associated protein); minimal spontaneous internalization. Antibody binding triggers phagocytosis by macrophages (ADCP) rather than classical endocytosis by target tumor cells themselves. Limited direct internalization by tumor cell. (I).

EGFR.evidence_note: EGFR (ErbB1) is highly expressed in NSCLC, HNSCC, and CRC. Depatuxizumab mafodotin (ABT-414) and MRG003 are key ADCs. Rapid internalization and high density, but significant on-target off-tumor toxicities (skin, ocular).
EGFR.internalization_mechanism: CME (CBL-mediated ubiquitination â†’ lysosomal degradation after EGF stimulation); at high antibody occupancy also macropinocytosis (I)
EGFR.recycling_after_internalization: Major recycling (Rab11-mediated recycling endosome) without ligand; degradation with EGF/ligand co-stimulation (I)
EGFR.shedding_rate: Low for full ECD; some EGFR domain shedding by ADAM10 (I)
EGFR.biomarker_assay: IHC (EGFR expression by IHC, H-score; EGFR mutation by NGS/PCR for subtype selection); EGFRvIII by specific IHC
EGFR.proliferation_sensitivity: High â€” most EGFR ADCs use MMAE/auristatin (mitosis-dependent); limits activity against slow-growing tumors
EGFR.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via CME (CBL-mediated ubiquitination â†’ lysosomal degradation after EGF stimulation); at high antibody occupancy also macropinocytosis (I).

EGFRvIII.evidence_note: EGFRvIII is a tumor-specific deletion mutant found in GBM. Depatuxizumab mafodotin (ABT-414) targeted this variant. High internalization and absolute specificity, but significant intratumoral heterogeneity in GBM.
EGFRvIII.internalization_mechanism: Rapid constitutive internalization (unlike WT EGFR, EGFRvIII internalizes without ligand due to constitutive activation) (I)
EGFRvIII.shedding_rate: Very low (tumor-cell-specific splicing; no detectable soluble form) (I)
EGFRvIII.biomarker_assay: EGFRvIII-specific IHC or RT-PCR (tumor-specific; not detectable in blood); high intratumoral heterogeneity is a major challenge
EGFRvIII.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid constitutive internalization (unlike WT EGFR, EGFRvIII internalizes without ligand due to constitutive activation) (I).

CD123.evidence_note: CD123 (IL-3Ra) is expressed in AML and BPDCN. Pivekimab sunirine (IMGN632) is a leading ADC. Rapid internalization and moderate density. Myelosuppression is the primary on-target toxicity.
CD123.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (>20% AML blasts CD123+; MFI-based quantification)
CD123.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” DGN462/IGN payloads (IMGN632) are cell-cycle independent
CD123.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid receptor-mediated endocytosis (IL-3RÎ±; clathrin-mediated) (I).

FLT3.evidence_note: FLT3 (CD135) is expressed in AML, particularly with ITD mutations. AGS62P1 (anti-FLT3-AS241) is a clinical-stage ADC. Rapid internalization and moderate density. DNA-damaging payloads are preferred for quiescent stem cells.
FLT3.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily degraded via ubiquitin-proteasome and lysosomal pathways (I)
FLT3.shedding_rate: Low (some sFLT3 shed, not clinically significant) (I)
FLT3.biomarker_assay: Flow cytometry (AML blasts); FLT3 mutation status (ITD/D835) by PCR/NGS for treatment selection
FLT3.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” most FLT3 ADC payloads are DNA-damaging (cell-cycle independent)
FLT3.design_logic: [Tier 2 (Active)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Ligand-induced rapid internalization (RTK pathway; clathrin-mediated) (I).

Mesothelin.evidence_note: High density in mesothelioma and ovarian cancer. Internalization POOR in most cell lines (key limitation limiting payload choice). Low normal tissue expression except pleural/peritoneal surfaces.
Mesothelin.internalization_mechanism: POOR â€” GPI-anchored protein primarily on apical/luminal surface; macropinocytosis is limited; SLOW internalization is a major ADC design challenge (I)
Mesothelin.recycling_after_internalization: Primarily membrane-retained; minimal degradation after internalization (I)
Mesothelin.shedding_rate: High â€” soluble megakaryocyte potentiating factor (MPF) shed by ADAM proteases; soluble mesothelin in plasma acts as antigen sink (reduces effective dose reaching tumor) (I)
Mesothelin.biomarker_assay: IHC (mesothelin expression by H-score >50); serum soluble mesothelin (SMRP) as pharmacodynamic biomarker
Mesothelin.proliferation_sensitivity: High â€” most Mesothelin ADCs use MMAE; poor internalization limits payload delivery efficiency
Mesothelin.design_logic: [Unknown Tier] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) moderate internalization. Internalized via POOR â€” GPI-anchored protein primarily on apical/luminal surface; macropinocytosis is limited; SLOW internalization is a major ADC design challenge (I).

FR_alpha.evidence_note: Validated in Phase 3 MIRASOL trial for ovarian cancer.
FR_alpha.internalization_mechanism: Receptor-mediated endocytosis (GPI-anchored; clathrin-independent, lipid raft pathway). Internalization is relatively slow but constitutive. (C)
FR_alpha.recycling_after_internalization: Significant recycling (~50% recycled within 4h). GPI-anchor promotes recycling endosome routing. (C)
FR_alpha.shedding_rate: Low â€” minimal soluble FRÎ± detected in serum of most patients. (C)
FR_alpha.biomarker_assay: IHC (FRÎ± â‰Ą75% tumor cells at 2+ intensity for mirvetuximab MIRASOL trial); VENTANA anti-FR alpha (SP130) RxDx is FDA-approved CDx.
FR_alpha.proliferation_sensitivity: Low â€” DM4 payload is cell-cycle dependent but FRÎ± recycling ensures continuous payload delivery.
FR_alpha._note: Alias for FRÎ± â€” see that entry for canonical data
FR_alpha.design_logic: [Tier 1 (Approved)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate_to_high density and Clinical (C) validation of high internalization via clinical data. Internalized via Receptor-mediated endocytosis (GPI-anchored; clathrin-independent, lipid raft pathway). Internalization is relatively slow but constitutive. (C).

GD2.evidence_note: High density in neuroblastoma (~1e7 copies/cell), TNBC. Internalization LOW to MODERATE (major challenge for ADC design). Normal expression on peripheral nerves and brain causes neuropathic pain.
GD2.internalization_mechanism: VERY SLOW â€” ganglioside (glycolipid); lipid raft-mediated macropinocytosis only; classical receptor endocytosis absent (I)
GD2.recycling_after_internalization: Minimal â€” lipid components recycled via lipid recycling pathways, not lysosomal delivery (I)
GD2.shedding_rate: Moderate (shed ganglioside in circulation; GD2 shedding rate correlates with tumor burden) (I)
GD2.biomarker_assay: IHC or immunofluorescence (â‰Ą20% cells GD2+); serum GD2 ganglioside as pharmacodynamic biomarker in neuroblastoma
GD2.proliferation_sensitivity: High â€” poor internalization means most therapeutic effect may rely on ADCC/CDC rather than payload delivery in GD2 ADCs
GD2.design_logic: [Tier 4 (Emerging)] Based on Inferred (I) high density and Inferred (I) low internalization. Internalized via VERY SLOW â€” ganglioside (glycolipid); lipid raft-mediated macropinocytosis only; classical receptor endocytosis absent (I).

gpNMB.evidence_note: Target for glembatumumab vedotin (CDX-011). Phase 2 data in melanoma showed activity.
gpNMB.internalization_mechanism: Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Glycoprotein non-metastatic B; localized to endosomes/lysosomes and cell surface). (I)
gpNMB.shedding_rate: Moderate â€” soluble gpNMB (sgpNMB) shed by ADAM10. (I)
gpNMB.biomarker_assay: IHC (gpNMB expression in melanoma and TNBC).
gpNMB.design_logic: [Tier 3 (Failed)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid clathrin-mediated endocytosis (Glycoprotein non-metastatic B; localized to endosomes/lysosomes and cell surface). (I).

AXL.internalization_mechanism: Rapid ligand (Gas6)-induced clathrin-mediated endocytosis. Constitutive in AXL-overexpressing tumors. (I)
AXL.shedding_rate: High â€” AXL ectodomain shed by ADAM10/17 releasing soluble AXL (sAXL); acts as antigen sink. (I)
AXL.biomarker_assay: IHC (AXL expression H-score); serum sAXL as PD biomarker.
AXL.design_logic: [Tier 3 (Failed)] Based on Inferred (I) moderate density and Inferred (I) high internalization. Internalized via Rapid ligand (Gas6)-induced clathrin-mediated endocytosis. Constitutive in AXL-overexpressing tumors. (I).

CAIX.internalization_mechanism: SLOW â€” Carbonic anhydrase IX is a transmembrane enzyme; minimal spontaneous internalization. Antibody binding triggers slow endocytosis. (I)
