mc-val-cit-PABC_MMAE.reason: Seagen platform; requires cathepsin B in endosome. MMAE is membrane-permeable â†’ bystander killing.

mc-val-cit-PABC_MMAF.reason: MMAF is charged â†’ no bystander effect. Requires high target density for efficacy.

GGFG_DXd.reason: DXd platform tolerates high DAR due to hydrophilic linker. Bystander effect addresses heterogeneity.

CL2A_SN38.reason: Moderate potency payload requires high DAR; pH-sensitive CL2A cleaves in acidic TME.

SMCC_DM1.reason: Non-cleavable; requires lysosomal degradation. DM1-lysine metabolite is charged â†’ no bystander.

Sulfo-SPDB_DM4.reason: Cleavable disulfide; DM4 metabolite is membrane-permeable â†’ bystander effect.

PEG-Val-Ala-PABC_PBD.reason: Extremely potent PBD dimer; low DAR mandatory. PEG spacer improves PK.

AcBut_Calicheamicin.reason: Acid-labile hydrazone; releases in lysosome. Legacy platform with hepatotoxicity risk.

VHL-cleavable_BRD4-PROTAC.reason: PROTAC payload degrades target proteins to overcome resistance. Large MW requires low DAR (1-2) and highly stable site-specific conjugation.

ISAC-Linker_TLR7/8 agonist.reason: Immune-stimulating ADC (ISAC). Activates myeloid cells in TME. Requires strict tumor specificity to avoid systemic cytokine storm.

TMALIN_TOP1i.reason: MediLink  Tumor Microenvironment Activable LINker. Dual cleavage (extracellular TME + intracellular). High stability in circulation.

antigen_internalization_to_linker._note: Matching antigen internalization rate to optimal linker cleavage mechanism.

heterogeneity_to_bystander._note: Tumors with high heterogeneity require bystander-capable payloads.

IgG1.use_when: Default for ADC. Strong Fc effector functions (ADCC/CDC) + long half-life.
IgG1.advantage: Well-characterized PK, FcRn recycling, established conjugation chemistry.

IgG4.use_when: When Fc-mediated effector function is undesirable (e.g., hematologic targets where ADCC would deplete normal cells).
IgG4.limitation: Fab-arm exchange risk (mitigated by S228P mutation).

Bispecific.use_when: Dual targeting to improve specificity or address resistance (e.g., HER2Ă—HER3, EGFRĂ—MET).

VHH_nanobody.use_when: Deep tumor penetration needed (solid tumors, brain), rapid clearance acceptable, renal excretion desired.
VHH_nanobody.advantage: Small size (15 kDa), excellent tissue penetration, can access cryptic epitopes.
VHH_nanobody.limitation: Short half-life (unless PEGylated or Fc-fused); limited DAR capacity.

Probody_Masked_ADC.use_when: Target is widely expressed on normal tissues (e.g., CD71, CD166); requires tumor-protease activation.
Probody_Masked_ADC.advantage: Expands druggable target space by protecting normal tissues.
Probody_Masked_ADC.limitation: Premature cleavage in circulation; masking peptide increases ADA (immunogenicity) risk; complex CMC.

payload_classification._note: Payload classes with potency, mechanism, and design implications.

tubulin_inhibitors.limitation: Resistance via MDR1 efflux pump (MMAE/DM1), peripheral neuropathy

topoisomerase_I_inhibitors.best_for: Heterogeneous tumors (bystander effect), moderate-to-low expression targets
topoisomerase_I_inhibitors.limitation: Diarrhea, ILD (interstitial lung disease) for DXd platform

dna_damaging_agents.best_for: Low-expression targets (extreme potency allows low DAR)

immunomodulators.best_for: Cold tumors, combination with checkpoint inhibitors
immunomodulators.limitation: Systemic immune activation if release is not controlled

ksp_inhibitors.mechanism: Inhibition of kinesin spindle protein â†’ mitotic arrest

bcl_xl_inhibitors.mechanism: Induction of apoptosis via Bcl-xL inhibition

protac_payloads.mechanism: Targeted protein degradation via E3 ligase recruitment
protac_payloads.best_for: Undruggable targets, overcoming resistance to small molecules

radionuclides.mechanism: Targeted radioligand therapy (RDC). Alpha emitters cause irreparable double-strand DNA breaks with short range (high energy, low bystander damage). Beta emitters have longer range (crossfire/bystander effect).
radionuclides.best_for: Radiosensitive tumors, heterogeneous tumors (Beta emitters), micrometastases (Alpha emitters).
radionuclides.limitation: Complex supply chain (isotope half-life), radiation dosimetry, delayed myelosuppression, nephrotoxicity.

protein_toxins.mechanism: Ribosome inactivation â†’ protein synthesis arrest

rna_polymerase_ii_inhibitors.mechanism: Inhibition of RNA Pol II â†’ transcription arrest
rna_polymerase_ii_inhibitors.best_for: Slow-growing or dormant tumors, p53-mutant tumors

spliceosome_inhibitors.mechanism: Inhibition of SF3B1 â†’ splicing disruption
spliceosome_inhibitors.best_for: Splicing-factor mutant tumors (e.g., SF3B1 mutant AML/MDS)

immune_stimulatory_agonists.mechanism: Innate immune activation (myeloid cells) in TME
immune_stimulatory_agonists.limitation: Cytokine release syndrome if systemically released

stochastic_cysteine.description: Partial reduction of interchain disulfides â†’ maleimide coupling
stochastic_cysteine.cmc_detail: High complexity in purification due to DAR distribution (0, 2, 4, 6, 8). Requires hydrophobic interaction chromatography (HIC) for characterization. Risk of maleimide de-conjugation via retro-Michael reaction in vivo.
stochastic_cysteine.fto_detail: Broad freedom to operate (FTO); core patents expired. Most widely used clinical method.

lysine_coupling.description: NHS ester reaction with surface lysines
lysine_coupling.cmc_detail: Extremely heterogeneous DAR distribution. Characterization requires mass spectrometry (native MS) and ion-exchange chromatography (IEX). High batch-to-batch variability risk.
lysine_coupling.fto_detail: Broad FTO; used in Kadcyla and Mylotarg.

site_specific_engineered_cys.description: THIOMAB: engineered cysteine at specific sites
site_specific_engineered_cys.cmc_detail: Requires precise control of reduction/re-oxidation to ensure engineered cysteines are reactive while native disulfides remain intact. High DAR homogeneity (typically DAR 2.0).
site_specific_engineered_cys.fto_detail: Patented by Genentech; requires licensing for commercial use.

enzymatic_transglutaminase.cmc_detail: Requires deglycosylation (PNGase F) or specific Q-tag insertion. Highly site-specific at Gln295. Excellent homogeneity. Minimal impact on antibody folding.
enzymatic_transglutaminase.fto_detail: Proprietary technology; AJICAP and NBE-Therapeutics hold key patents.

glycan_remodeling.description: GlycoConnect: enzymatic remodeling of Fc N297 glycan â†’ azide coupling
glycan_remodeling.cmc_detail: Two-step enzymatic process: (1) Trimming of native glycans, (2) Addition of azide-modified sugars for click chemistry. High homogeneity (DAR 2 or 4). Bypasses need for protein engineering.

AJICAP.description: Affinity peptide-directed site-specific conjugation at Fc Lys248
AJICAP.cmc_detail: Uses a temporary affinity peptide to direct conjugation to Lys188/248. No protein engineering required. High homogeneity for native IgG.

unnatural_amino_acid.description: Amber codon suppression â†’ pAzF/pAcF incorporation â†’ click chemistry
unnatural_amino_acid.cmc_detail: Requires cell line engineering (orthogonal tRNA/RS pair). Allows precise placement of payload anywhere on the scaffold. High manufacturing complexity (COGS).

enzyme_fge_smartag.description: Formylglycine-generating enzyme (FGE) converts Cys to aldehyde for HIPS ligation

sortase_a_mediated.description: Enzymatic ligation using Sortase A at LPETG motif

tub_tag_tubulin_ligase.description: Tubulin tyrosine ligase (TTL) adds unnatural tyrosine derivatives

iLDC_ligase_GeneQuantum.description: Intelligent Ligase-dependent Conjugation (iLDC) using proprietary ligase for site-specific coupling.

thiobridge_polytherics.description: Disulfide bridging conjugation maintaining antibody stability

snap_tag.description: Engineered SNAP-tag protein fusion for benzylguanine derivatives

ajicap_v2.description: Second generation AJICAP without Fc affinity peptide

formylglycine_smartag.description: FGE enzyme conversion of Cys to fGly

glycoconnect_synaffix.description: Native glycan remodeling and click chemistry

ThioBridge.cmc_detail: Payload bridges the two sulfur atoms of a reduced disulfide bond. Improves ADC stability compared to maleimide. Maintains structural integrity of the antibody.

GeneQuantum_iLDC.cmc_detail: Enzymatic conjugation using microbial transglutaminase or other ligases in a continuous flow process. High efficiency and scalability.

safety_rules._note: Hard safety constraints for ADC design.

dar_potency_balance.rule: DAR Ă— IC50 product must stay within therapeutic window

ADC_plus_Immune_Checkpoint_Inhibitor (PD.rationale: ADC induces Immunogenic Cell Death (ICD), releasing neoantigens and activating dendritic cells, turning 'cold' tumors 'hot', synergizing with PD-1 blockade.
ADC_plus_Immune_Checkpoint_Inhibitor (PD.risk: Overlapping immune-related adverse events (irAEs), particularly Interstitial Lung Disease (ILD) or pneumonitis.

ADC_plus_Anti_Angiogenic (VEGF).rationale: Anti-VEGF (e.g., Bevacizumab) normalizes tumor vasculature, reducing interstitial fluid pressure, which significantly enhances the penetration of large ADC molecules into solid tumors.

ADC_plus_PARP_Inhibitor.rationale: DNA-damaging payloads (TOP1 inhibitors like DXd/SN-38, or PBDs) create DNA breaks. PARP inhibitors block DNA repair, creating synthetic lethality.

ADC_plus_TKI.rationale: Overcome resistance bypass pathways (e.g., HER2 ADC + EGFR TKI).

risk_factors: High DAR with hydrophobic payloads (hapten effect)
ADA_immunogenicity_risk.mitigation: Hydrophilic linkers, fully humanized binders, site-specific conjugation to shield payload.

cmc_manufacturing_rules._note: CMC and manufacturability constraints for ADC design.

preferred_characteristics.hydrophobicity: HIC profile should show single dominant peak

hydrophobic_payload.risk: High DAR with hydrophobic payload â†’ aggregation
hydrophobic_payload.mitigation: Use hydrophilic linker (PEG spacer, GGFG), reduce DAR, add surfactant

site_specific_dar2.limitation: May need ultra-potent payload to compensate for low DAR

decision_tree_logic._note: Step-by-step decision algorithm for the recommendation engine.

step_1_disease_to_antigens.action: Look up disease_antigen_map â†’ get primary and secondary antigens with tiers

step_2_antigen_to_properties.action: Look up antigen_properties â†’ get density, internalization, heterogeneity, off-tumor risk

step_3_properties_to_linker_payload.action: Apply compatibility rules: internalizationâ†’linker_type, heterogeneityâ†’bystander_requirement, densityâ†’DAR_range

step_4_safety_constraints.action: Apply safety_rules: DARĂ—potency check, off-tumor risk, class toxicity warnings

step_6_conjugation_technology.action: Select conjugation method from conjugation_technology based on homogeneity needs and patent freedom

step_7_precedent_check.action: Query adc_master_internal.json for existing approved/clinical ADCs with same target

step_8_rank_and_report.action: Score by: clinical_precedent (30%), safety_profile (25%), CMC_feasibility (20%), FTO_freedom (15%), differentiation (10%)

binding_affinity.purpose: Measure KD to recombinant antigen and cell-surface antigen. Too high affinity may cause 'binding site barrier' effect.
binding_affinity.protocol_summary: Standard SPR: Immobilize antigen on CM5 chip; inject ADC at 5-6 concentrations (0.1-100 nM). Calculate Ka, Kd, KD. Compare Intact ADC vs Parental mAb.
binding_affinity.cell_line_recommendations: Target-overexpressing (e.g., SK-BR-3 for HER2, BT-474) vs Target-negative (e.g., MDA-MB-231 for HER2).
binding_affinity.readout_sensitivity: KD down to pM range for SPR; FACS requires ~10^4 receptors/cell.

internalization.purpose: Quantify rate and extent of ADC trafficking into acidic endosomes/lysosomes.
internalization.protocol_summary: pHrodo labeling: Conjugate ADC with pH-sensitive dye; incubate with cells at 37Â°C; monitor fluorescence increase (lysosomal entry) via FACS or live-cell imaging over 24h.
internalization.readout_sensitivity: t1/2 detection from 15 mins to 48 hours.

cytotoxicity.purpose: Determine IC50 on Target-positive vs Target-negative cell lines to prove specificity.
cytotoxicity.protocol_summary: CellTiter-Glo: Seed cells (3000/well); add ADC (10-point dilution, 10 nM to 1 fM); incubate 72-120h; add CTG reagent; measure luminescence. Calculate IC50.
cytotoxicity.cell_line_recommendations: Panel of 5-10 lines with varying antigen density (H-score 0 to 300).

methods: Co-culture assay (Target+ and Target- cells with different fluorescent reporters)
bystander_effect.purpose: Verify if released payload can kill neighboring antigen-negative cells (crucial for heterogeneous tumors).
bystander_effect.protocol_summary: Mix GFP+ (Target+) and RFP+ (Target-) cells at 1:1 ratio. Add ADC. Monitor RFP+ cell death over 5 days. If RFP+ cells die, payload is membrane-permeable.
bystander_effect.cell_line_recommendations: HER2+ SK-BR-3 mixed with HER2- MDA-MB-231.

methods: LC-MS/MS after incubation in human/cyno/mouse plasma
plasma_stability.purpose: Measure payload shedding rate in circulation (Free payload vs Intact ADC).
plasma_stability.protocol_summary: Incubate ADC in human/mouse plasma at 37Â°C for 0, 1, 3, 7 days. Extract samples; analyze via LC-MS/MS to quantify intact ADC vs free payload.
plasma_stability.readout_sensitivity: LOQ (Limit of Quantitation) typically 1-10 ng/mL for free drug.

linker_cleavage.purpose: Confirm payload release mechanism and kinetics.
linker_cleavage.protocol_summary: In vitro enzyme assay: Incubate ADC with recombinant Cathepsin B (pH 5.0) or Î˛-glucuronidase. Analyze payload release kinetics via HPLC/MS.

efficacy_models.purpose: Evaluate tumor growth inhibition (TGI) and survival in immunocompromised mice.

pharmacokinetics_pk.purpose: Determine half-life, clearance rate, and systemic exposure of the toxic payload.

toxicology_tox.purpose: NHP is critical if the antibody cross-reacts with monkey antigen. Evaluates on-target off-tumor tox and payload-driven tox (e.g., bone marrow, liver).

antigen_internalization_profile._description: Maps antigen internalization/shedding profiles to linker and payload class recommendations

rapid_low_shedding.dar_guidance: DAR 3â€“8 well-tolerated depending on linker hydrophilicity
rapid_low_shedding.bystander_requirement: Not required but beneficial for heterogeneous targets

rapid_high_shedding.dar_guidance: Higher DAR (6â€“8) preferred to overcome antigen sink effect; only feasible with hydrophilic linkers (GGFG, PEG variants)
rapid_high_shedding.bystander_requirement: REQUIRED â€” bystander killing compensates for reduced payload delivery from shed antigen
rapid_high_shedding.special_note: Consider target proximal epitope binding to minimize shed antigen competition

slow_low_shedding.dar_guidance: Higher DAR (6â€“8) beneficial to maximize payload from limited internalization events
slow_low_shedding.bystander_requirement: REQUIRED â€” slow internalization must be compensated by high bystander effect to kill neighboring tumor cells
slow_low_shedding.special_note: Tubulin inhibitors (MMAE) are typically SUBOPTIMAL for poor internalizers â€” limited payload reaches lysosome

recommended_payload_classes: Immune Stimulatory Agonists (ISAC â€” TME activation)
slow_moderate_shedding.special_note: Glucuronide linker advantage: TME extracellular beta-glucuronidase also cleaves linker, providing bystander delivery even without internalization

rapid_recycling.dar_guidance: DAR 3â€“4 (high-DAR targets with recycling risk ADC clearance)
rapid_recycling.special_note: Recycling reduces net payload per cell â€” pulse dosing or fractionated dosing strategies may help

payload_selection_rules._description: Rules for matching payload class to clinical/tumor context

high_ki67.rationale: High proliferation index means most cells are in S/G2/M â€” tubulin inhibitors maximally effective

acceptable_payloads: Tubulin Inhibitors (reduced efficacy but still active)
low_ki67.rationale: Low proliferation = most cells in G0/G1 â€” tubulin inhibitors require cells to enter mitosis; TOP1i and DNA alkylators work independently of cell cycle

preferred_payloads: Topoisomerase I Inhibitors (DXd â€” strong bystander effect + cell-cycle independence)
Immune Stimulatory Agonists (ISACs â€” TME activation)
acceptable_payloads: Tubulin Inhibitors with PEG-vc linker (moderate bystander)
avoid_payloads: PBD dimers (excessive off-target toxicity in heterogeneous tumors)
heterogeneous.rationale: Heterogeneous antigen expression requires strong bystander killing â€” cell-cycle independent DXd kills antigen-negative neighbors

preferred_payloads: Immune Stimulatory Agonists (TLR7/STING â€” reactivate innate immunity)
Topoisomerase I Inhibitors (immunogenic cell death via IFN pathway)
immunosuppressive_TME.rationale: Immunosuppressive TME reduces T-cell dependent efficacy; ISAC approach + DXd-type immunogenic death can reprogram TME

avoid_payloads: ISACs (CRS risk in already inflamed tumors)
inflamed_TME.rationale: Inflamed TME: direct cytotoxic payloads work well; ISAC may cause excessive cytokine release

liquid_tumor_AML.first_choice_payload: DNA Damaging Agents (calicheamicin â€” Mylotarg precedent; cell-cycle independent for LSCs)
liquid_tumor_AML.second_choice: RNA Pol II Inhibitors (alpha-amanitin â€” targets quiescent LSCs)
liquid_tumor_AML.avoid: PBD dimers (unacceptable hepatotoxicity/VOD at therapeutic doses in clinical setting)
liquid_tumor_AML.rationale: AML blasts include quiescent LSCs â€” cell-cycle independent payloads essential for cure

liquid_tumor_lymphoma.first_choice_payload: Tubulin Inhibitors (MMAE â€” Adcetris precedent in CD30+ lymphoma)
liquid_tumor_lymphoma.second_choice: DNA Damaging Agents (PBD â€” Zynlonta in DLBCL)
liquid_tumor_lymphoma.rationale: Lymphoma cells are rapidly cycling â€” tubulin inhibitors highly effective

liquid_tumor_myeloma.first_choice_payload: Tubulin Inhibitors (MMAF â€” Blenrep precedent in BCMA+)
liquid_tumor_myeloma.second_choice: Topoisomerase I Inhibitors (cell-cycle independent for post-mitotic PCs)
liquid_tumor_myeloma.rationale: Plasma cells are largely post-mitotic â€” MMAF has reduced bystander effect (important to reduce hematological toxicity)

solid_tumor_breast.first_choice_payload: Topoisomerase I Inhibitors (DXd â€” Enhertu/Trodelvy revolution)
solid_tumor_breast.second_choice: Tubulin Inhibitors (MMAE â€” Kadcyla, Padcev precedent)
solid_tumor_breast.avoid: PBD dimers (severe myelosuppression, no clinical validation in breast)
solid_tumor_breast.rationale: High tumor proliferation + HER2/TROP2 expression â€” DXd provides bystander + cell-cycle independence for heterogeneous expression

solid_tumor_gastric.first_choice_payload: Topoisomerase I Inhibitors (DXd â€” RC48, CMG901 for CLDN18.2)
solid_tumor_gastric.second_choice: Tubulin Inhibitors (MMAE â€” telisotuzumab vedotin variant)
solid_tumor_gastric.rationale: Gastric cancer often has slow internalization (CLDN18.2, FGFR2b) â€” DXd bystander effect critical

solid_tumor_lung_NSCLC.first_choice_payload: Topoisomerase I Inhibitors (DXd â€” DESTINY-Lung, TROPION-Lung)
solid_tumor_lung_NSCLC.avoid: PBD dimers (interstitial lung toxicity risk compounds ILD risk from DXd class â€” do not combine)
solid_tumor_lung_NSCLC.rationale: NSCLC often heterogeneous HER2/TROP-2/HER3 â€” DXd bystander effect overcomes heterogeneity

solid_tumor_SCLC.first_choice_payload: DNA Damaging Agents (IGN/DGN class â€” IMGN632 precedent via SCLC neuroendocrine biology)
solid_tumor_SCLC.avoid: PBD dimers at high DAR (Rova-T TAHOE failure â€” excess toxicity)
solid_tumor_SCLC.rationale: SCLC is fast-cycling but Rova-T failure showed PBD at high DAR is too toxic; lower-DAR site-specific conjugation with PBD may be revisited

linker_selection_rules._description: Rules for linker selection based on antigen internalization, tumor type, and payload

rapid.rationale: Rapid internalization â†’ payload quickly reaches lysosome â†’ Cathepsin B cleavage efficient â†’ vc-PABC and GGFG both excellent

moderate.rationale: Moderate internalization â†’ GGFG with high DAR (7â€“8) maximizes delivery per binding event; glucuronide provides extracellular TME release as backup

slow.rationale: Slow internalization â†’ glucuronide linker provides dual mode: lysosomal AND extracellular TME cleavage. GGFG at high DAR maximizes payload per sparse internalization event.

high_shedding.rationale: Shed antigen acts as sink â€” compensate by maximizing payload loaded per antibody (high DAR). Hydrophilic linkers essential at high DAR.
high_shedding.special_note: Target membrane-proximal epitope to reduce competition from shed ectodomain

low_shedding.rationale: Low shedding â†’ standard DAR 3â€“4 approaches are effective; can use thioether non-cleavable when lysosomal degradation is confirmed sufficient

tubulin_inhibitors_MMAE.notes: MMAE requires free amine release from PABC spacer; GGFG not directly compatible with MMAE (GGFG is optimized for DXd chemistry)

tubulin_inhibitors_MMAF.notes: MMAF charged â€” no bystander effect desired; maleimide-Cys conjugation with peptide spacer

TOP1_inhibitors_DXd.notes: GGFG is the proprietary Daiichi Sankyo linker optimized specifically for DXd release; alternative tetrapeptide linkers are being developed

TOP1_inhibitors_SN38.notes: SN-38 carbonate ester conjugation (Trodelvy CL2A linker); pH-sensitive or glucuronide cleavage both viable

DNA_damaging_calicheamicin.notes: Acid-labile hydrazone â†’ requires lysosomal pH release; disulfide component adds GSH-mediated release

DNA_damaging_PBD.notes: PBD dimers require site-specific low-DAR conjugation; vc-PABC or VA-PABC for Cys-specific attachment

radionuclides_alpha.notes: Not a chemical linker â€” requires specific macrocyclic chelator for each radiometal; no organic linker applies

immune_stimulatory_ISAC.notes: ISACs require controlled payload release in endosome/lysosome; hydrophilic linkers prevent aggregation with agonist payloads

design_axioms.title: Bystander Effect is Mandatory for Heterogeneous or Slow-Internalizing Targets
design_axioms.rule: IF antigen heterogeneity == 'high' OR internalization_rate == 'low' OR internalization_rate == 'very_low' THEN payload MUST have bystander_effect == 'Yes' (membrane-permeable payload)
design_axioms.consequence: CONTRAINDICATED: MMAF (charged, no bystander) for heterogeneous targets
design_axioms.evidence: DXd-ADC advantage in HER2-low: bystander DXd kills HER2-negative neighbors. Enhertu outperforms T-DM1 partially due to this.

design_axioms.title: High-Shedding Targets Require High-DAR + Hydrophilic Linker
design_axioms.rule: IF antigen shedding_rate == 'high' OR shedding_rate == 'very_high' THEN DAR_target >= 6 AND linker MUST be hydrophilic (GGFG, PEG-vc, glucuronide)
design_axioms.consequence: Standard DAR 3â€“4 with hydrophobic linker (vc-PABC) will be neutralized by shed antigen sink at high tumor burden
design_axioms.evidence: Shed BCMA (sBCMA) correlates with belantamab resistance. MUC16/CA125 shedding renders low-DAR ADCs subtherapeutic in high-burden ovarian cancer.

design_axioms.title: Quiescent Tumor Cells Require Cell-Cycle Independent Payloads
design_axioms.rule: IF tumor_type in ['myeloma', 'CLL', 'prostate_mCRPC', 'low_grade_lymphoma'] OR target_cell_is_leukemic_stem_cell THEN payload MUST have cell_cycle_dependency == 'ALL phases'
design_axioms.consequence: SUBOPTIMAL: MMAE/MMAF for quiescent plasma cells (myeloma), LSCs (AML), or slow-cycling prostate cancer
design_axioms.evidence: Calicheamicin (cell-cycle independent) is the backbone of Mylotarg success in AML. DXd (ALL phases) outperforms MMAE in low-proliferation contexts.

design_axioms.title: Non-Cleavable Linkers Require Confirmed Lysosomal Delivery
design_axioms.rule: IF linker is non-cleavable (Sulfo-SMCC, thioether) THEN antigen internalization_rate MUST be 'high' or 'rapid' AND recycling_after_internalization MUST be predominantly 'degraded'
design_axioms.consequence: Non-cleavable linker for slow-internalizing target = payload never released = no efficacy
design_axioms.evidence: Kadcyla (T-DM1, non-cleavable SMCC) works because HER2 is rapidly internalized and degraded. CD20 ADC with non-cleavable linker failed due to minimal internalization.

design_axioms.title: DAR >4 Requires Hydrophilic Linker
design_axioms.rule: IF DAR_target > 4 THEN linker hydrophilicity MUST be 'high' (PEG-vc, GGFG, glucuronide, PEG8-vc)
design_axioms.consequence: DAR >4 with hydrophobic linker (standard vc-PABC, SMCC) causes ADC aggregation â†’ rapid FcÎłR-mediated clearance â†’ reduced tumor exposure + increased liver toxicity
design_axioms.evidence: DXd high-DAR (7â€“8) enabled by GGFG's high hydrophilicity. PEG8-vc-PABC allows DAR 6â€“8 without aggregation.

design_axioms.title: log P > 3 Payload at DAR > 4 Requires PEG Spacer
design_axioms.rule: IF payload log_p > 3 AND DAR_target > 4 THEN linker MUST include PEG (â‰ĄPEG4) spacer to offset hydrophobicity
design_axioms.consequence: High log P payload (MMAE cLogP ~3.2, cryptophycin cLogP ~3.5) at high DAR creates hydrophobic ADC that aggregates and clears rapidly
design_axioms.evidence: PEG4-vc-PABC and PEG8-vc-PABC developed specifically to enable high-DAR MMAE ADCs without aggregation.

design_axioms.title: ILD Risk Surveillance Mandatory for DXd-Class Payloads
design_axioms.rule: IF payload is TOP1i (DXd, SN-38, exatecan) THEN clinical protocol MUST include ILD monitoring (CT scan every 2 cycles; corticosteroid protocol for Grade 2+ ILD)
design_axioms.consequence: DXd-class ILD (interstitial lung disease) occurs in ~10â€“15% of patients; can be fatal if Grade 3â€“4; early detection and corticosteroid treatment is life-saving
design_axioms.evidence: Enhertu ILD fatal in 2.6% (DESTINY-Breast01). FDA-mandated REMS program for T-Dxd.

design_axioms.title: Radionuclide ADC Requires Chelator Stability Assessment
design_axioms.rule: IF payload is radionuclide THEN chelator-metal stability in human serum MUST be confirmed (>95% intact at 72h); free metal release > 5% is a safety failure
design_axioms.consequence: Free Pb2+, Th4+, or Bi3+ release causes systemic metal toxicity (renal, bone marrow); chelator stability is the primary safety determinant
design_axioms.evidence: DOTA-Pb212 stability: clinical TAT programs require serum stability assay before IND.

design_axioms.title: PBD Dimer at DAR > 2 is Contraindicated in Most Solid Tumors
design_axioms.rule: IF payload is PBD dimer AND DAR > 2 AND indication is solid tumor THEN CONTRAINDICATED based on clinical evidence (Rova-T TAHOE failure)
design_axioms.consequence: PBD at DAR 2 already causes hepatotoxicity and myelosuppression at effective doses; DAR > 2 compounds non-specific delivery to normal tissues
design_axioms.evidence: Rova-T (DLL3-PBD, DAR 2): TAHOE Phase 3 stopped for excess toxicity at PBD DAR 2. Clinical experience strongly limits PBD use to hematologic malignancies with site-specific low-DAR conjugation.

design_axioms.title: Bispecific ADC Format Preferred for Antigen Heterogeneous Solid Tumors
design_axioms.rule: IF antigen heterogeneity == 'high' AND bystander effect is insufficient to overcome heterogeneity THEN consider bispecific binder format (targets two antigens simultaneously)
design_axioms.consequence: Bispecific ADC (e.g., HER2Ă—HER3, EpCAMĂ—HER2) captures both antigen+ and antigen-low cells via avidity; reduces resistance from antigen-low subclones
design_axioms.evidence: MCLA-128 (HER2Ă—HER3 bispecific) Phase 2: captures acquired heregulin-driven HER3 upregulation that escapes trastuzumab.

design_axioms.title: CD47 and Non-Internalizing Targets are Poor ADC Candidates â€” Use ISAC or BiAb Instead
design_axioms.rule: IF antigen internalization_rate == 'very_low' AND primary_mechanism is NOT ADCC/ADCP THEN ADC format is NOT recommended; consider ISAC (for TME activation) or bispecific T-cell engager
design_axioms.consequence: Non-internalizing target prevents lysosomal payload release â€” ADC achieves only surface binding without cytotoxic activity
design_axioms.evidence: CD20 ADC failures (SAR3419) largely attributed to poor internalization of rituximab-type binding. CD47 has essentially no internalization.
